entry.title: Re: Unleash Your Inner ABAP Rockstar with Joule for developers , ABAP AI capabilities
entry.content:
Whilst I think its great that Joule for ABAP is released and available to paying customers of BTP (that follow the note above) I really think this needs to be opened up to all as soon as possible.

My reasoning. Only paying customers of BTP will get this technology, which lets not forget is a tool to assist developers to do their everyday jobs. For bloggers to generate interesting blogs around this topic, today, they would then be blogs relating to a customer solution. Now for my customer, usually we are not allowed to blog about topics related to customer specific solutions (legally). Adoption is lower if you cannot blog about a topic...

As I see it - Joule for developers, is a bit like copilot for Microsoft platforms. Copilot is released to anyone with a 365 license or so I understand it. This means there is a very large user base, and a lot of people blogging. I think as Joule is restricted, and as blogging about customer solutions is kinda a grey area for legality, I suspect we may not see a lot of really good blogs just yet. Not until Joule is available more widely (ideally openly available on BTP trial instances).

entry.title: Best Practices for Network Connectivity in a Multi-Site Global Organization
entry.content:
Hi everyone,

I have a customer with multiple sites worldwide, and I want to understand the best practice for setting up VPN connectivity.

  • Should each site establish its own VPN tunnel to the central network (multiple site-to-site VPNs)?
  • Or is it recommended to have a single centralized VPN tunnel that routes all traffic through a central hub?

I’d appreciate insights on the most efficient and secure approach for SAP connectivity in such scenarios.

Thanks!

entry.title: Optimizing Shutdown, Turnaround, and Outage Management with SAP sto360 by WIPRO
entry.content:
Optimizing Shutdown, Turnaround, and Outage Management with SAP sto360

In asset-intensive industries such as Oil & Gas, Energy, Chemicals, Mining, and Utilities, planned shutdowns, turnarounds, and outages (STOs) are critical and highly complex events. The ability to execute these projects effectively and efficiently plays a significant role in the profitability of an asset. sto360 is an end-to-end solution for managing all aspects of asset turnaround events. It is the only SAP Endorsed App designed to revolutionize STO planning, execution, and optimization

What is SAP sto360?

SAP sto360 is an advanced, cloud-enabled solution that extends SAP’s Enterprise Asset Management (EAM) capabilities, specifically addressing the unique challenges of STO projects. By integrating seamlessly with SAP S/4HANA and SAP Enterprise Asset Management (EAM), Asset Performance Management (APM), Project System (PS), Material Management (MM), and External Scheduling application as an option, sto360 enables organizations to gain real-time visibility, improve planning and collaboration, and drive information-driven decision-making throughout the entire STO lifecycle.

Key Features and Benefits

  1. End-to-End STO Management
    SAP sto360 provides a unified platform for planning, scheduling, and executing STOs, ensuring alignment between maintenance teams, procurement, and operations.
  2. Real-Time Analytics and Insights
    Advanced analytics powered by SAP’s intelligent technologies help organizations predict potential risks, optimize resource allocation, and improve execution efficiency.
  3. AI-Driven Decision Support
    SAP sto360 harnesses artificial intelligence to provide intelligent recommendations, automate repetitive tasks, and enhance decision-making.  AI-powered insights address potential bottlenecks, optimize resource utilization, and streamline STO execution for maximum efficiency.
  4. Digital Workflows and Automation
    The application eliminates manual processes by digitizing workflows, reducing paperwork, and ensuring compliance with industry regulations.
  5. Predictive Maintenance Integration
    sto360 leverages IoT and SAP APM predictive maintenance capabilities to identify asset conditions in advance, reducing unplanned downtime and improving overall equipment reliability.
  6. Improved Cost Control and ROI
    Through optimized planning and information-driven decision-making, SAP sto360 minimizes budget overruns and enhances the return on investment for STO projects.

Why Choose an SAP Endorsed App?

SAP Endorsed Apps are premium certified solutions that meet the highest standards of integration, security, and performance. SAP sto360 is built to enhance SAP’s ecosystem, ensuring a seamless and scalable STO management experience.

Transform Your STO Execution with SAP sto360

With SAP sto360, organizations can shift from reactive to proactive STO management, improving STO operational efficiency and effectiveness, reducing risks, and ensuring that critical assets operate at peak performance. If you’re looking to optimize your shutdown, turnaround, and outage processes, now is the time to explore how SAP sto360 can drive transformation for your business.

Join us at SAP for Energy & Utilities Conference, scheduled for April 8-10, 2025, in Rotterdam, Netherlands to learn more and meet our customers and partners. 

Want to learn more? Contact us or visit SAP’s Endorsed Apps Marketplace to explore sto360 in detail.

entry.title: Re: Is SYBASE Mainframe Connect v15 compatible with CICS...
entry.content: Since these products do not have any OS and CICS dependences, they are expected to work on the newer CICS and z/OS versions. The MFC products for CICS use IBM's CICS Command Level and CICS Socket Interface API's. Therefore the products are isolated from the OS and CICS versions.

entry.title: Create SAML2.0 External Request in ABAP
entry.content:
I am trying to develop an integration scenario which requires a call to an external Electronic Identification service using SAML 2.0 protocol. I need to create an XML message in eIDAS format, which should be signed using SAML protocol, in order to make an authetication request.

This is a request message example provided by the WebService (which is called Cl@ve 2.0) .

entry.title: Re: Difference between “Assigned Restriction Types” and “...
entry.content:
Hi @Olli76 ,

 

The subject of Roles and Authorizations is a bit messy/convoluted right now in IBP, but to keep it simple:

- you would use "Key Figure Restrictions" when you want to allow access to the Key Figure, regardless of the attributes (example planners will be able to change Actuals Qty, regardless of the region/country/customer).

- you would use "Permission Filter" + specify an attribute, when you want to be more restrictive in terms of access (example planners will be able to change Actuals Qty only for the attribute you have mentioned in the profile, eg Region = EU/US etc)

entry.title: SAP User Experience Q1/2025 Update – Part 4: SAP Ariba, SAP Fieldglass
entry.content:
This fourth post in my series gives an overview of latest UX innovations in SAP Ariba and SAP Fieldglass, products for managing your spend (procurement) and external workforce. For SAP Ariba: the new Intake Management application and news for Category Management. For SAP Fieldglass, the highlight is the new Home Page, but we’ll also look at further UX improvements.

 

SAP Ariba

The recent UX innovations I’d like to present here are:

  • The new SAP Ariba Intake Management application, with its great UX.
  • UX enhancements in Category Management: Initiatives Overview and allowing category managers to store attachments.

SAP Ariba Intake Management

Rather than having to go to many different systems to manage small and large procurement needs, from simple purchase requests to onboarding new service providers, SAP Ariba Intake Management gives you your “one stop shop” for all of a purchasers procurement requests. It provides a fast, simple, and intuitive single front door for all procurement requests, streamlining processes and shielding users from system complexity. This saves time, and avoids the frustration of dealing with multiple systems for ordering and tracking status.

Joule is also at hand to guide users, making it even easier.

Figure 1: SAP Ariba Intake Management. ALT Text: The image shows a screenshot of the “My Requests” overview, with a “Recently Requested” section at the top, with flat cards for “Request Software”, “Request Supplier”, “Request Sourcing Event”, “Return Items”, “Request Unlisted Software” and “…”. Below that an “Overview” section with subsections for “In Progress” with cards for Software Requests, showing status “In Progress” and IT Service with status “Pending” and “Completed”. Then a further subsection “Completed” with two rows of cards, the first four are “Purchase Requisition”, “Facility Service”, “Carfleet Service”.

There is much more to it than shown above in Figure X, so do have a look at it in action:

  • Video: SAP Ariba Intake Management (1:39 min.)
  • Video: Elevate user experiences with SAP Ariba Intake Management (6:51 min.)
  • Blog post: SAP Ariba Intake Management: A Seamless, User-Centric Solution with Powerful Extensibility.

SAP Ariba Category Management

Initiatives Overview

Management reporting of category management initiatives got easier with the Initiatives Overview application (Figure 2). Users managing or analyzing multiple categories can have a bird’s eye view of all initiatives in a single place. The customizable interface with multiple filters, interactive charts, and a Gantt chart view, can help users to gain insights for their tasks, whether they are strategic, like monitoring the benefits towards their procurement goals, or tactical, like planning initiatives execution order due to dependencies or balancing the team workload.

Figure 2: Initiatives Overview in SAP Ariba Category Management, with different visualization options. ALT Text: The image shows two screenshots of the Initiatives Overview. On the left a visualization with four vertical bar charts for the last four quarters showing planned, active, completed and obsolete in different colors. Below that a list of Initiatives, with category, region, assignee (including a photo), start and end date and status with coloured badges. On the right a Gantt chart visualization of the same initiatives, with a visual filter bar at the top, and below that the Gantt chart showing the time period from May to September.

Attachments

With attachments, category managers can upload files of different formats and add links to their category strategy and plan documents. The benefits of being able to do that are many, including: referencing an important source of information that drove some of their strategic decisions, ease the access to documents like policies, laws, and regulations, and even extend the SAP Ariba Category Management capabilities by adding their own analysis tools as slides, spreadsheets, or PDFs.

Figure 3: Attachments in SAP Ariba Category Management. ALT Text: The image shows two screenshots from the application “Develop Strategy and Plan”: on the left the mai page, with four steps shown as horizontal flow at the top (1. Category Analysis, 2. Market Analysis, 3. Strategy and Plan, 4. Attachments and Approval). Section 4 is selected, showing a card on the left with the Approval Flow, showing that the item is approved. On the right a card listing four attachments (Vendor List, a contract PDF,…). The screenshot on the right shows a popover with a preview of the contract PDF file, with buttons for “Download” and “Close”.

Get an overview of SAP Ariba Category Management, as well as What’s New:

  • SAP Ariba Category Management.
  • What’s New in SAP Ariba Category Management.

 

SAP Fieldglass

I’m happy to introduce the new Home Page, along with the layout manager to ensure that each user sees what is relevant to them. Another recent innovation is the new characteristics tab for Statements of Work. Finally, we’ll look at some improvements for administrators.

New Home Page

The highlight here is the new home page. It uses UI5 integration cards which are designed to focus on what’s most important for the user, guided by insights from recent user research. Figure 4 shows what it looks like.

Figure 4: New SAP Fieldglass home page. ALT Text: A screenshot with a banner at the top saying “Hi, Jennifer! Welcome to SAP Fieldglass”, containing a “What’s New” button on the right and a settings button. Below that a section with tab “Pinned Links” selected, along with tabs for “Recently Viewed Items” and “Starred Items”. Below that the page is filled with cards relevant for the user, such as “My Work Items”, showing the type and number of items of that type, “Let’s help you get started”, “Announcement” with some internal information about important actions that need your attention, a card “My Workers” listing five workers with image, name, ID, end date and health indicator, and a card “My Job Posting” listing two postings (Senior Developer and Junior Developer), each with status “Submitted”, and showing the next steps (“Supplier needs to respond” and “Review Responses” respectively).

Layout Manager

The new layout manager allows administrators to customize the content visibility based on user roles, allowing for a tailored experience that aligns with each role’s specific needs and responsibilities. We provide default layouts, and customers can create their own ones, as you can see in Figure 5.

Figure 5: New Layout Manager in SAP Fieldglass. ALT Text: Two screenshots: top left the Layout List, showing four default layouts name and description (Administrator Layout; Finance and Reporting Layout; Hiring Manager Layout; PMO Layout). Bottom right the Hiring Manager Layout page configuration is shown, with a list of cards showing name and description (My Workers; My Interviews; My Job Postings; In Progress Items).

Characteristics Tab in SOW (Statement of Work)

By highlighting the status and payment details of line items, users can quickly assess project progress and spending. This makes it easier to track which items are completed, in progress, or pending, helping users make informed decisions, avoid overspending, and prioritize tasks effectively.

It also improves communication with stakeholders by providing clear financial and status insights.

Figure 6: The SAP Fieldglass characteristics tab for SOW (Statement of Work). ALT Text: A screenshot of the SOW Dashboard, for an item “Building #45 Renovation – Holdbacks”. The header shows the progress graphically (three of five steps are green), the status (Approved), ID, Period and Vendor. Below that the page has numerous tabs, the “Payment Line Items2 tab is selected, showing two cards at the top: “Committed Amount” and “Payment Line Items Status”, showing that there are 6 items in total, with one pending approval, two invoiced and three created. Below that a table is shown, displaying four items, one in each row, with columns for status, sequence number, name, owner, PO number, requested and final amount.

Improvements for Administrators

In addition to the new home page, we have introduced a pinned links section on the administrator pages. This new feature enables users to quickly access their “favorite” items, such as frequently used or particularly important items, streamlining their workflow and enhancing overall efficiency.

Figure 7: Pinned Admin Links in SAP Fieldglass. ALT Text: A screenshot of the “Admin Configuration” with a section at the to for “Pinned Admin Links”, containing three links (icon plus text): “Create Cost Center”; “View: Business Units”; “View: Cost Centers”. Below that the page shows a number of cards in four columns, each containing links. The cards shown are “User”, “Financial Data”, “Services”, “Report”, “Supplier”, “Integration” – each containing between three and more than ten links.

Administrators also benefit from the new self-service dashboard for company configuration changes (not visualized here). With this, buyers gain autonomy with expanded configuration options within their company setup, eliminating the necessity of reaching out to support for updates. This enhancement offers clients greater flexibility and control over their settings.

 

Continued soon…

I hope you enjoyed this fourth part, covering SAP Ariba and SAP Fieldglass.

I will post part five next week, covering latest UX innovations for selling with SAP Customer Experience and SAP Industry Cloud – Retail.

Part 1 of the series lists all the parts, and links to those parts that have already been published:

  • SAP User Experience Q1/2025 Update – Part 1: Many New Innovations Available (AI, Joule and More).

Do keep posting your experiences and recommendations yourself in the SAP Community, with the SAP Fiori and/or the User Experience tag! In case you are wondering how to get a list of the most recent blog posts on SAP Fiori and User Experience in our new SAP Community, use these links:

  • Latest blog posts on SAP Fiori in the SAP Community.
  • Latest blog posts on User Experience in the SAP Community.

For general information on design at SAP and SAP Fiori, check out:

  • www.sap.com/design.
  • www.sap.com/fiori    
  • SAP Community: SAP Fiori topic page.

entry.title: Re: Is there a way to detect whether a BAPI is for read or write through metadata?
entry.content: @Sandra_Rossi Classifying BAPIs as read or write is necessary because, when a write BAPI executes successfully, we want to call transaction commit to save the changes. Keeping track of whether a particular BAPI is read or write helps ensure this is handled correctly

entry.title: Re: SBO Mailer from Opportunity screen, not showing the m...
entry.content:
Hello,

I don't know for sure. But it seems to me that this is a business rule of the system. Because when you have a sales order, you wan't to send it to your customer, but when you just have the opportunity, you are still in negotiation, so Sales Opportunity is more a document for your internal control, and because of that you will want to send the email "by default" to the owner of this negotiation. 

Anyway, you can still manually add the customer to receive the e-mail.

Best regards

entry.title: 📣‌ SAP Emarsys: Q1 2025 Product Release 📣‌
entry.content:
Happy New Year! 🥂 

I know the end of February may be a bit late to still be saying that, but this is technically my first post of 2025, so I’m going to relish saying it one last time!

Each new year inevitably brings change, excitement and opportunity for improvement. It’s on that note that I'm pleased to share the highlights from an already busy and exciting start to 2025 at SAP Emarsys.  

But first, if you noticed a slightly different look and feel about Emarsys, you’re not just imagining things. To kick off the year, we rebranded to SAP Emarsys including a fresh new logo, color palette, and imagery. But it’s not just cosmetic, we’ve applied the SAP style throughout the product UI and experience and even moved our product documentation to the SAP Help Portal to take advantage of the powerful capabilities of the knowledge base. Read more about our rebrand here. 

What hasn’t changed is our commitment to helping our customers deliver omnichannel excellence at scale and achieve business outcomes. So, with that let’s dive into our Q1 product release, jam-packed with impactful updates and innovative new features to increase productivity, simplify delivering exceptional customer experiences, and help you generate revenue and results more efficiently. 

Extensible, Scalable Platform Updates 

To deliver meaningful customer engagements in the moments that matter, you must be able to connect, manage, and activate your data from source and to any channel. Our latest extensible platform updates help you do just that: 

Connect to data from anywhere: 

  • Batch Sales Data API (Pilot): Simplify large scale sales data loads, now with self-service configuration and data quality monitoring tools 
  • Engagement Events (Pilot): Connect to events from any source to power segmentation, personalization and real-time marketing engagements 
  • Account Engagement: B2B marketers using SAP Emarsys and Sales Cloud can now see a simplified overview of account data and begin to target key groups with the Account Fact Sheet and Account-based Segmentation 

Activate across your ecosystem: 

  • Digital Ads with Criteo: Build ad audiences from SAP Emarsys data and activate on commerce and content publisher sites 
  • Mercaux: In-Store Tactics: Notify store associates about key customer events to take action 

Additionally, we’ve rolled out several features which will help customers get the most out of SAP Emarsys whether your business is expanding or you’re already operating at scale. 

  • Consent Management Tool: Gives admins control to review and accept terms to enable key features, like the popular AI-Assisted Subject Line Generator 
  • Asset Tagging: Offers an efficient way to categorize and filter your assets  
  • Embedded SAP Support Ticketing: Enables SAP customers to open and manage support tickets directly from SAP Emarsys 
  • Accelerated Adoption Services Package for SAP Emarsys: A new offering with dedicated experts to help you make the most of your investment 

Intelligent, Omnichannel Innovations 

Omnichannel and personalization remain at the heart of what we do, which is reflected in many of the updates in this release enhancing marketers’ capacity to execute their strategies and engage with customers how and where they prefer to interact.  

Content & Channel Innovations 

  • Omnichannel Content Blocks: Our new standard for building and distributing content across channels adds support for In-app messaging and a new Brand Center (pilot) 
  • Mobile Messaging: Updates include enhanced segmentation with Mobile App & SMS data plus the ability to use Multiple Short Codes in a Single Account  
  • Mobile Wallet: Scale campaign creation with a Copy Campaign button, segment based on Mobile Wallet data (pilot), and push notifications when passes are updated (pilot) 
  • Conversational Channel: Support more languages with use of the IETF field, expand use cases with support for Mobile Wallet campaigns, add interactivity with CTA buttons for WhatsApp, engage in dynamic dialogue with Conversational Messaging (pilot), and measure the impact with revenue attribution 

AI-powered intelligence and personalization 

  • AI-Assisted Product Finder (Pilot): Utilize natural language search to curate the right products for your campaign 
  • AI-Assisted Report Builder (Pilot): Query your data and build ad-hoc reports with the help of generative AI 
  • Email Domain Reporting: Get improved visibility into domain performance and spot potential deliverability issues with a refreshed report UI and new functionality 

 

Did I mention that the Product & Engineering teams have been busy? If you'd like to learn more about these features, how they work, and the value you can realize: 

  • Read the full product release blog 
  • Explore the full details on the product release page 
  • Register for the Q1 2025 Emarsys Release Webinar on February 27th  

And if you are interested in joining one of the active pilots or early adopter programs, please submit your request or reach out to your SAP Customer Success Partner (CSP) or SAP Emarsys Customer Success Manager (CSM).

entry.title: Re: Notas SAP para a Reforma Tributária
entry.content:
Olá a todos.

Aplicamos as duas notas já liberadas, referente à Reforma Tributária: 3552901 e 3552903.

A nota 3552901, dentre outras coisas, inclui 4 novos IDs de Grupo de Impostos no domínio J_1BTAXGRP (IBSS, IBSM, CBS e IS).
Mas quando o cliente possui o módulo de Oil, nosso caso, este domínio possui um append (OIH_CV_BR_J_1BTAXGRP_FVA).
Neste caso a nota se perde e acaba excluindo IDs pré existentes (WACO, WACS, WAIR e II).

Pelo o que entendi, a rotina move todos os IDs originais 4 posições para baixo, para inserir os novos no topo. No entanto ocorre uma sobreposição dos 4 últimos do domínio J_1BTAXGRP, os mesmos citados acima (WACO, WACS, WAIR e II), com os primeiros do append OIH_CV_BR_J_1BTAXGRP_FVA. É nesse momento que o programa se perde e estes acabam ficando de fora.

Mais alguém passou por isso? A SAP tem conhecimento deste problema? Busquei nota e não encontrei nada.
Agradeço desde já.

Em tempo: a versão do nosso produto é SAP_APPL 618, SP 08.

Agradeço desde já qualquer ajuda.

entry.title: Re: Why on earth doesn't HANA BINTOTEXT() work on STXL.CL...
entry.content: I believe the data is stored in a compressed format (no idea of what internally the system uses). Its effectively a BLOB and only the application knows how to decode and process the actual data stored in it, so you have to go through the READ_TEXT FM to process it to return it as an internal table of lines of text. The way it is processed internally by the system it may be stored in the DB as multiple rows with the same ID depending on the size of the text being stored.

entry.title: Xsuaa and approuter in hybrid testing: POST request completed w/status 401 Authentication required
entry.content:
Hello!

 

I am hybrid testing a project where I am using xsuaa and approuter. However, I get this error in approuter log: POST request to /odata/v4/account/callExternalAPI completed with status 401 Authentication required
Here the configurations:
 

 

 

 

 

Service definition
service AccountService {
    
    entity TestEntity {
        key ID : UUID;
        Name   : String;
    }

    action callExternalAPI() returns String;
}<div><div><span>CDS configuration for hybrid testing in package.json project file:<li-code lang="json">{
...
  "cds": {
    "requires": {
      "auth": "xsuaa",
      "destinations": true,
      "[hybrid]": {
        "auth": "xsuaa"
      }
    }
  }
}<div><div><span>xs-secutiry.json project file:<li-code lang="json">{
  "xsappname": "testvscode1",
  "tenant-mode": "dedicated",
  "scopes": [],
  "attributes": [],
  "role-templates": [],
  "authorities-inheritance": true,
  "oauth2-configuration": {
    "redirect-uris": [
      "http://localhost:5001/"
    ]
  }
}<p> <span>default-env.json approuter file:<li-code lang="json">{
    "PORT": 5001,
    "destinations": [
      {
        "name": "srv-api",
        "url": "http://localhost:4004",
        "forwardAuthToken": true
      }
    ]
  }

 

 

 

 

 

xs-app.json approuter file:

 

 

 

 

{
    "authenticationMethod": "none",
    "routes": [
      {
        "source": "^/app/(.*)$",
        "target": "$1",
        "localDir": ".",
        "cacheControl": "no-cache, no-store, must-revalidate"
      },
      {
        "source": "^/appconfig/",
        "localDir": ".",
        "cacheControl": "no-cache, no-store, must-revalidate"
      },
      {
        "source": "^/odata/v4/account/(.*)$",
        "target": "/odata/v4/account/$1",
        "destination": "srv-api",
        "csrfProtection": false
      },
      {
        "source": "^/(.*)$",
        "target": "$1",
        "destination": "srv-api",
        "csrfProtection": false
      }
    ]
  }

 

 

 

 

To run the test I am using the following commands:
- cds bind --exec -- npm start --prefix app/router --> to execute approuter
- cds watch --profile hybrid --> to execute the developed CAP service
 
I execute these requests in Postman:
- https : // <org> . authentication . us10 . hana . ondemand . com / oauth / token to get the bearer token
- http : // localhost : 5001 / odata / v4 / account / callExternalAPI to call the action, including the token from the previous request
 
 
 
However, I the error mentioned below:
#2.0#2025 02 20 09:50:13:982#-03:00#ERROR#/Handler#####1452e28d-b331-4350-98be-2eab93f6ed6f####Sc8PKx7C2gRnIK6lc1gQnwHU4MmHRGd2######m7dcayxl#PLAIN##POST request to /odata/v4/account/callExternalAPI completed with status 401 Authentication required#
 
What am I missing in the configuration?

Thanks for your time and help.

Regards!

Mig

entry.title: Re: How the ABAP Test Cockpit supports you to adopt ABAP Cloud
entry.content:

we want to setup central ATC on BTP to scan all connected S/4 and cloud systems.

I assume this is caused by Language Version of package S_CODE_Inspector has "Standard ABAP" but our main package has "ABAP for Cloud Development".

So we're not able to activate all checks which we used to have in onPrem central ATC.

Is there any possibility to have full scope of checks available for our custom check variants ?

Thanks a lot !

Volker

entry.title: Re: Easy Price Maintenance in SAP Business ByDesign
entry.content:
Hello,

 

Please note that the suggested number of line items in this Blog do not apply and my cause considerable performance issues.

 

For optimum performance, the number of line items in a Price List or Discount List should be limited to around 10000 or less. When you check the affected Price List, you can see that the number of line items exceeds 10000 and hence, the error occurs.

 

See also the following relevant KBA’s for this Topic:

KBA 3079750 - Error When Opening, Downloading, Extracting or Querying Price List or Discount List

KBA 2614379 - Error Faced During Price List Upload

KBA 3460889 - Unable to Release Price Lists Consistently When the Number of Items over 10000

KBA 2218922 - Cannot Migrate Price List When the Price List is Too Huge

 

Best Regards,

Kathrin

entry.title: Please vote 341529 -Request to bring Latest People Profile/Full Profile Categories into RBP purview
entry.content:
Hi All, 

Currently in the Latest People Profile's Full Profile, there is no way to control/manage Categories and Cards in the RBP. This is unnecessarily showing Categories and Cards, especially not relevant for the users' themselves and also when users' seeing others' data, they see all categories and cards too. They may not have access to see the data, but this is a very poor design, user experience and going backwards. This will cause unnecessary confusion to the users and effort to admins in terms of queries. 

Please vote customer influence ID 341529 if you think this will benefit your company in this journey. There is some cards related suggestions as well. 

https://influence.sap.com/sap/ino/#/idea/341529/?section=sectionDetails

Regards - DRP

SAP SuccessFactors Platform   SAP SuccessFactors Employee Central  #SUCCESSFACTORS ROLES

entry.title: massenlöschung von veralteten Rollen
entry.content:
Hallo,

ich muss massenweise veraltete Rollen löschen, jedoch funktioniert das ganze nicht so ganz.

Über die Transaktion pfcgmassdelete kann ich zwar einige Rollen löschen, bekomme aber diese Fehlermeldung, wenn bestimmte Rollen in den mitgegeben Werten enthalten sind:

Ich habe über die agr_1250 Tabelle herausgefunden, dass einige Rollen beim Objekt S_CODE öfters das gleiche Profil hinterlegt haben.

In manchen Fällen funktioniert es dann, wenn ich in der pfcg in die Rolle reingehe,  "Expertenmodus zur Profilgenerierung -> alten Stand lesen und mit den neuen Daten abgleichen" klicke, und die Rolle dann generiere. Nach diesen Schritten sind die Rollen neu abgemischt und ich kann sie dann ohne weitere Probleme löschen.

Jedoch gibt es da zwei große Probleme:
1. sind es extrem viele Rollen, sodass es keinen Sinn macht das händisch zu machen

2. dieses Vorgehen funktioniert nicht bei allen Rollen, die den S_TCODE mehrfach vergeben haben.

Meine Frage ist also, kennt jemand einen Weg, um massenweise Rollen abzumischen? Hatte schonmal jemand mit ähnlichen Problemen zutun gehabt? Weiß jemand warum das abmischen bei manchen Rollen funktioniert und bei manchen nicht?

Bin für jede Hilfe dankbar.

entry.title: Beyond SAP Cloud - Episode 3: SAP BTP guidance framework
entry.content:
In this episode we introduce the SAP Business Technology Platform's guidance framework, which offers various PDF guides and documentation tailored to different personas, including architects, developers, and administrators. While these resources provide valuable insights, it doesn’t capture everything. It can serve as a start point or a base line and it is definitely an interesting source and read for resources working on SAP BTP.

Takeaways

  • SAP Business Technology Platform provides guidance through various PDFs and documentation.
  • Guidance is available for multiple personas, such as Architects, Developers, and Administrators.
  • Different guides cater to different roles within the SAP ecosystem.
  • It's recommended to explore the guidance framework for a solid foundation in SAP technologies.
  • The SAP BTP guidance framework can be found here: What is the SAP BTP Guidance Framework? | SAP Help Portal
  • The guidance framework doesn't capture everything; it serves as a starting point.
  • Consider other sources of information to complement the guidance framework.
  • We are hosting sessions and workshops to help customers understand the content more deeply.

entry.title: Re: Implementing BAPI_CONTRACT_CHANGE
entry.content:
This is old but adding information here because we went through the same.

The answer is in function module documentation for EXTENSIONIN parameter.

The fields added by Fiori Custom fields app are added to a different structure.

Example, to populate item customer fields

DATA: 
  lt_extensionin TYPE bapiparex_tab, 
  lt_ctitem_custfields TYPE STANDARD TABLE OF mmpur_oa_bapi_item_ext, 
  lwa_ctitem_custfields TYPE mmpur_oa_bapi_item_ext. 

* After filling LT_CTITEMCUSTFIELDS 

*Set data of item custom fields in extensionin 
TRY. 
  cl_cfd_bapi_mapping=>get_instance( )->map_to_bapiparex_multi( 
     EXPORTING ir_source_table = REF #( lt_poitem_custfields ) 
     CHANGING ct_bapiparex = lt_extensionin ). 
CATCH cx_cfd_bapi_mapping. 
  CLEAR lt_extensionin[]. 
ENDTRY.

Pass LT_EXTENSIONIN to the BAPI

entry.title: Re: How to show the Story Filter value in a Textbox in SAC?
entry.content:
Hi,

Yes, this is possible. The key is understanding how .getDimensionFilters() works. If ‘All’ is selected in the story filter, it returns a zero-length array. If any specific values are selected, it returns an array of length 1 containing a filter object. To access the selected values, you need to cast the filter object to either SingleFilterValue or MultipleFilterValue before you can access .value or .values. Although these casted objects have a .description property, it unfortunately remains an empty string (perhaps a bug), so it is necessary to then call .getMember() for the actual description.

Using a dynamic text element in a text box to display the story filter natively is more responsive, but the scripted approach allows greater customisation of how the text is displayed. For example, both the native dynamic text and the scripted approach preserve the order in which users select values. If they should always be sorted alphabetically, memberNames.sort() can be used before joining them. The separator can also be modified by adjusting .join(), or the approach can be changed entirely to display a count, such as “17 Business Units” instead of listing each name.

If your list of Business Units is not too long, performance could be improved by calling .getMembers() during story initialization to store all member IDs and descriptions in global variables. This would avoid repeated costly .getMember() calls each time the filter changes.

Here’s a script that will correctly update a textbox:

 

var displayText = "";

// Retrieve the dimension Story Filter.
// Do this on Application.getFileDataSource() as getDataSource() on a widget won't return the Story filter
var dimensionFilters = Application.getFileDataSource("YOUR_DATASOURCE_ID")
    .getDimensionFilters("BUSINESS_UNIT_DIMENSION");

// If 'All' is selected, the array will be empty
var dimensionFilter = dimensionFilters[0];

if (dimensionFilter === undefined) {
    displayText = "All Business Units";
} else {
    // Now cast to the appropriate filter type before .value or .values can be accessed.
    switch (dimensionFilter.type) {
        case FilterValueType.Single:
            var singleValue = cast(Type.SingleFilterValue, dimensionFilter);
            
            // Retrieve description via getMember() since .description is empty
            displayText = this.getDataSource()
                .getMember("BUSINESS_UNIT_DIMENSION", singleValue.value)
                .description;
            break;

        case FilterValueType.Multiple:
            var multipleValues = cast(Type.MultipleFilterValue, dimensionFilter).values;
            var memberNames = ArrayUtils.create(Type.string);

            for (var i = 0; i < multipleValues.length; i++) {
                var memberDescription = this.getDataSource()
                    .getMember("BUSINESS_UNIT_DIMENSION", multipleValues[i])
                    .description;
                memberNames.push(memberDescription);
            }

            // Sort alphabetically before joining (optional)
            memberNames.sort();
            displayText = memberNames.join(", ");
            break;
    }
}

// Apply the text to the textbox
Text_1.applyText(displayText);

 

You would add this script to the onResultChanged event of an appropriate widget.

For additional detail on .getDimensionFilters() see: https://help.sap.com/docs/SAP_ANALYTICS_CLOUD/00f68c2e08b941f081002fd3691d86a7/646826977afe4709af322461823b7872.html?locale=en-US&q=getDimensionFilters 

Cheers, Ivan.

entry.title: Sap Fiori Elements Tooltip for a field with a unit of measure is it possible?
entry.content:
Hi Everyone,

I have a sap CAP Fiori Elements app, and in the object page I have a field that we would like to have a tool tip on it. My field in the Object Page looks originally like this, and it is a field with a unit of measure (using the annotation @Measures.Unit)

 

{
   $Type: 'UI.DataField',
   Value: sctcd,
}

 

this type does not support the annotation @Common.QuickInfo, I have found a work around for this using 

 

{	
   $Type : 'UI.DataFieldForAnnotation',
   Target: '@UI.DataPoint#fieldWithTooltip',
}

 

and 

 

UI.DataPoint #fieldWithTooltip: {
     Value               : sctcd,	
    ![@Common.QuickInfo]: 'tooltip text', 
}

 

as described in https://github.com/SAP-samples/fiori-elements-feature-showcase?tab=readme-ov-file#adding-a-field-with-a-tooltip-to-a-table.

However, when using this work around, the unit of measure field stop working as expected because does not display the help option anymore.

Any options to achieve this using Fiori Elements?

Thanks,

Tatiana F.

entry.title: Re: How to show the Story Filter value in a Textbox in SAC?
entry.content:
Just checked, and you can definitely make this substantially faster by preloading all Business Unit values during initialisation. One approach is to store them in two separate string arrays: one for the keys and one for the descriptions. When needed, you can find the index of a key in the first array and use it to access the corresponding description in the second array.

A cleaner approach I have used in other situations is to repurpose the PlanningModelMember datatype for a global script variable. To use this datatype, you first need to add a planning model script object to your story, but you don’t actually need to assign a planning model. This allows you to efficiently store and retrieve Business Unit descriptions without repeatedly calling .getMember(). If using the PlanningModelMember approach, your initialisation script would look something like this, assuming you have created the script variable BUs with type PlanningModelMember:

if (BUs === undefined) {
    // First need to initialise the variable to access .properties
    BUs = cast(Type.PlanningModelMember, {
        id: "1",
        properties: {
            INIT: "" // Placeholder property for initialisation
        }
    });
}

var buMembers = Chart_1.getDataSource().getMembers("BUSINESS_UNIT_DIMENSION", {limit: 1000});

for (var i = 0; i < buMembers.length; i++) {
    // Leverage the .properties object to store the descriptions by key
    BUs.properties[buMembers[i].id] = buMembers[i].description;
}

Once the key:description pairs have been stored in the properties object of the BUs variable during initialisation, the onResultChanged script can be significantly condensed. Instead of calling .getMember() repeatedly, we can simply retrieve the descriptions directly from BUs.properties, making the script significantly more efficient. The updated script would look like this:

var displayText = "";

// Retrieve the dimension filter
var dimensionFilters = Application.getFileDataSource("YOUR_DATASOURCE_ID")
    .getDimensionFilters("BUSINESS_UNIT_DIMENSION");

// If 'All' is selected, the array will be empty
var dimensionFilter = dimensionFilters[0];

if (dimensionFilter === undefined) {
    displayText = "All Business Units";
} else {
    switch (dimensionFilter.type) {
        case FilterValueType.Single:
            var singleValue = cast(Type.SingleFilterValue, dimensionFilter);
            
            // Retrieve description from preloaded properties
            displayText = BUs.properties[singleValue.value];
            break;

        case FilterValueType.Multiple:
            var multipleValues = cast(Type.MultipleFilterValue, dimensionFilter).values;
            var memberNames = ArrayUtils.create(Type.string);

            for (var i = 0; i < multipleValues.length; i++) {
                // Retrieve description from preloaded properties
                memberNames.push(BUs.properties[multipleValues[i]]);
            }

            // Sort alphabetically before joining (optional)
            memberNames.sort();
            displayText = memberNames.join(", ");
            break;
    }
}

// Apply the text to the textbox
Text_1.applyText(displayText);

By using the preloaded BUs.properties object, this approach eliminates the costly .getMember() API calls, making the overall result just as fast as the native Dynamic Text approach in my testing. Cheers, Ivan.

entry.title: Streamlining Goods Movements from Reservations
entry.content:
Introduction

Efficiently managing goods movement is crucial for maintaining smooth operations in any business. With the latest development in reservations, you can now expedite your goods movement processes by posting a goods movement directly from a reservation. You can do this using the app Manage Material Documents for Reservations (App ID: F7279). 

Key Features 

The Manage Material Documents for Reservations app offers several functionalities to enhance your goods movement processes: 

  • Direct Posting: Post a goods issue directly from a reservation for supported movement types, such as Goods Issue to Cost Center (201) and Goods Issue for Order (261). 
  • Bulk Processing: Post goods issues for multiple items simultaneously, saving time and effort. 
  • Draft Management: Manage drafts of material documents to ensure accuracy before final posting. 

How-To Post a Goods Movement from a Reservation

Starting in the Manage Reservation Items app, choose a reservation with movement type 201 or 261. With the Create Goods Movement button, you will navigate to the Manage Material Documents for Reservations app where you can post your goods issue. 

Step-by-Step Procedure: 

  1. Select a Reservation: Begin by finding a reservation with a supported movement type, such as 201. 
  2. Initiate Goods Movement: Click on 'Create Goods Movement' from the table toolbar. 
  3. Adjust Details: In the Manage Material Documents for Reservations app, adjust the quantity if needed and add a storage location. 
  4. Add Items: To include additional items, click 'Add'. A pop-up will display possible reservation items. Select and add them to your material document. 
  5. Enhance Details: For each line item, you can add short text or serial numbers if applicable. Click 'Apply' to save changes. 
  6. Finalize Posting: Once all details are confirmed, click the 'Post Goods Issue' button to complete the process.

By following these steps, you can ensure a seamless and efficient goods movement process directly from reservations.

Click the image below to see the process in a how-to video:

entry.title: How is the Output Channel of a Purchase Order Determined?
entry.content:
When generating purchase orders output documents, whether they are created/changed/canceled for a specific supplier, the default output channel remains printer/fax/email, despite being configured as 'Do Not Send'.


------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
Learn more about the SAP Support user and program here.

entry.title: Re: How is the Output Channel of a Purchase Order Determi...
entry.content:
Cause: Output channel of a Purchase Order is determined in one of the below methods which are mentioned priority wise:

  1. The output channel you manually selected in purchase order via You can also -> Edit Output Settings for Created/Changed/Cancelled PO
  2. The output channel you maintained for purchase order in the respective supplier master data
  3. The output channel you maintained in Application and User Management, Output Channel Selection for Created/Changed/Cancelled PO

Resolution:

1.If you manually changed the output channel in purchase order via You can also -> Edit Output Settings for Created/Changed/Cancelled PO, system gives highest priority to it.

2.If no manual changes at purchase order level, system determines the output channel according to what you maintained for purchase order in the respective supplier master data, Communication tab, Collaboration subtab.

Note: In supplier master data, the output channel maintained for purchase order would valid to all of the Created/Changed/Cancelled PO. You can't specify channels separately.

3.If no output channels maintained for purchase order for the respective supplier, system determines the output channel according to the selection rule you maintained in Application and User Management, Output Channel Selection for Created/Changed/Cancelled PO.

You can also refer to KBA: https://me.sap.com/notes/2678740
